messages.content: Several risk write-ups may describe the same underlying risk. Pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) and fold any UNIQUE early-warning indicators from the others into it. If they are genuinely different risks, keep the strongest as exemplar anyway only when they truly overlap — otherwise return the highest-scored id with no folded indicators and note they are distinct.

content: {"keepId":"risk.boundary-substitution.colo-follows-hyperscale-spec","rationale":"Both risks attack the same underlying flaw: the model's colocation slice (0.18) and hyperscale slice (0.44) both rest on rescaled GLOBAL Synergy shares that don't hold at CE scope, and hyperscale demand actually flows through colo halls with tenant-specified fit-outs. The colo-follows-hyperscale-spec write-up scores higher (18 vs 17) and has the sharpest mechanism — it identifies the precise contractual path (wholesale lease hands rack-power spec to the hyperscale tenant) by which the excluded substitution walks through the colocation door, plus it carries no sensitivity band to absorb it. The second risk's core insight (global-split mirage, weight belongs in colocation) is a mirror of the same procurement-path problem and reinforces rather than contradicts the keeper. I fold in its two distinct measurement indicators (self-build vs colo GW split, country-level ownership mix) which add early-warning coverage the keeper lacks.","foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Europe hyperscale self-build vs colocation GW split, updated quarterly","where":"CBRE 'Europe Data Centres' quarterly figures report; DCD coverage of CBRE releases","threshold":"CE-7 hyperscale self-build share reported <30% of CE capacity","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Country-level ownership mix (colocation/hyperscale/enterprise) for DE and NL","where":"EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' annual report country tables","threshold":"Germany capacity shown as majority colocation-operated","updates":"increases"}]}
